what_it_does: A static HTML/CSS desktop mockup of the right-hand Inbox review surface, showing a completed self-build (status 'implemented') for a new 'sentiment' node type. It renders the BuildIntentCard with a demonstrate-first outcome headline, a gated 'show me it working' button, collapsed changed-files record, derived-from provenance, and operator-only accept/send-back controls.
observations: The file is a full-page app mockup with top toolbar, left process/content rail, central canvas, footer, and right review panel.
The right panel shows the Inbox tab active with a 'decision → build' lane containing one BuildIntentCard.
The card is marked 'bi-done' with phase 'implemented ✓' and tag '⚙ build-intent'.
The headline section is '✓ done — here is what you can now do' followed by a prominent '▷ show me "sentiment" working' button.
A 'derived from' provenance line lists decision://inbox/route-by-tone, intent://build/sentiment-node, and proposal://brain/0xa1.
The changed-files diff and build summary are placed in a collapsed 'for the record' disclosure.
The verdict area has 'accept' and 'send back' buttons plus a 'why' textarea and an 'operator-only — an agent can never self-approve' guard.
The left rail includes a 'fresh' chip for the newly registered 'sentiment' node type.
The canvas contains a placeholder explaining that the demo button will drop a runnable node there.
Extensive HTML comments map UI elements to source locations in runtime/suite.py, App.tsx, and governance.py.
standouts: Demonstrate-first review design: the working outcome and demo button are the headline; the diff is secondary and collapsed.
Explicit operator-only resolve guard surfaced directly in the UI.
Demo button is gated on the new node type being live in the registry, per the grounding notes.
Provenance is shown as a 3-part bind (decision/intent/proposal) rather than hidden metadata.
conventions: Uses CSS custom properties from ../design-system.css for theming.
Uses data-ui-ref attributes to label UI elements with URI-like references.
Uses CSS grid named areas (top, rail, canvas, foot, panel) for layout.
Includes inline comments that document real backend strings, statuses, and source line ranges.
Commented-out expanded diff markup is used to evidence the collapsed structure.
concerns: The expanded diff content is commented out inside the live markup, which could be mistaken for active content if uncommented accidentally.
Some data-ui-ref values contain spaces and slashes that may not conform to a strict identifier scheme.
Placeholder counts like '3 file(s) changed' are hard-coded rather than generated.
notes: This is the desktop design artifact for the post-build review moment, where the operator validates a self-built node by seeing it work and can only accept or send it back.
questions: Are the accept/send-back buttons in this mockup wired to actual api.resolve calls, or are they purely visual?
How does the 'show me it working' button place and execute the new node on the canvas?
Are all referenced CSS custom properties defined in design-system.css?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose in the HTML comments (WIRE-review and INB-resolve for a demonstrate-first build review) matches the rendered content.
intention_signals: The file is trying to define the review interaction so an operator validates a self-built outcome by observing it work, with clear authority to accept or reject and a visible chain of provenance.
novelty: It inverts the typical code-review UI by making the diff a collapsed footnote and the 'show me it working' button the central, gated action, while also surfacing an operator-only approval guard.
contribution: A concrete desktop mockup and interaction pattern for a post-build review card that centers demonstration over diff-reading, exposes provenance, and enforces operator-only resolution.
summary_for_embedding: Desktop HTML mockup of a demonstrate-first build-result review card in an Inbox panel, showing an implemented self-build for a new 'sentiment' node with operator accept/send-back controls.
intention_for_embedding: To design the operator review moment for a self-built node, emphasizing live demonstration, provenance, and operator-only resolution.
